data.title: Trump's Remarks Influence U.S. Interest Rate Expectations
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, on January 17, data from CME FedWatch indicates that traders have reduced their expectations for two U.S. interest rate cuts in 2026. This shift follows U.S. President Donald Trump's suggestion that he may nominate someone other than National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett to succeed Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell.

By the end of 2026, the probability of no rate cuts throughout the year stands at 11.8%. The likelihood of a cumulative 25 basis point cut is 30.3%, while a 50 basis point reduction is anticipated at 32.1%.

Today, Trump commented on Hassett, stating, "I want him to remain in his current position, and we will see." This statement implies that Trump may have another candidate in mind for the Federal Reserve Chair position.

data.title: Trump's decision to drop Hassett has significantly reduced expectations for a Federal Reserve rate cut.
data.description: U.S. Treasury prices fell as Trump hinted at nominating someone other than National Economic Council Director Hassett to succeed Powell, with traders cutting their expectations for two U.S. rate cuts in 2026. The decline in U.S. Treasuries pushed the two-year yield up as much as 5 basis points to 3.61%, the highest level since the Fed's last rate cut in December. Short-term interest rate contracts reflected a decreased probability of two 25-basis-point rate cuts by the Fed this year after Trump's comments on Hassett. Meanwhile, the Treasury market continued to be troubled by the December jobs data released a week earlier, prompting Wall Street banks that had previously predicted a rate cut at the Fed's next meeting on January 28 to abandon that view. Morgan inflation economists predict that despite the change in Fed leadership, the Fed will not cut rates further. John Fath, managing partner of BTG Pactual Asset Management U.S., said, "The previous trade was betting that whoever becomes the next Fed chairman will be dovish. That has reversed in the last few days." (Jinshi)

data.title: U.S. Supreme Court Expected to Announce Ruling on January 20
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the U.S. Supreme Court is anticipated to release at least one decision on January 20, which falls on a Tuesday.

data.title: Market forecasts indicate that Warsh is the frontrunner for the position of Federal Reserve Chairman, while Hassett is significantly lagging behind.
data.description: According to data from the prediction market Polymarket, Hassett's probability of being nominated for the Fed Chair has dropped to 15%, on par with Federal Reserve Governor Waller, while Kevin Warsh's probability has risen to over 60%, making him the frontrunner and far ahead of other candidates. Today, when Trump spoke about Hassett, the director of the White House National Economic Council, he said, "We'll see if he stays in his current position." This statement suggests that Trump may have someone else in mind for the Fed Chair nomination, and market expectations for the Fed Chair nomination have shifted from a two-way race between Kevin Hassett and Kevin Warsh to Warsh leading alone. It's worth noting that Hassett himself commented on this before Trump's speech today, saying, "Wash and Rieder would also be great Fed Chairs." Perhaps Hassett already knew Trump's final choice.

data.title: Federal Reserve Governor Bowman Comments on Inflation Levels
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, Federal Reserve Governor Bowman stated that the current underlying inflation level is closer to the Federal Reserve's 2% target.

data.title: Federal Reserve Makes Significant Progress in Reducing Inflation
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, Federal Reserve Governor Michelle Bowman stated that the Federal Reserve has made considerable progress in reducing inflation.

data.title: Over $77 million in liquidations occurred across the entire network in the past hour, with $33.17 million in BTC liquidations.
data.description: According to Coinglass data, $77.59 million in positions were liquidated across the network in the past hour, including $2.21 million in short positions and $75.37 million in long positions. In addition, $33.17 million in BTC positions were liquidated and $11.6 million in ETH positions were liquidated.

data.title: Bowman defies the trend and advocates for interest rate cuts; analysts say his future vote could be crucial.
data.description: Analyst Adam Button stated, "It was initially thought that Bowman might return to a hawkish stance after her failed bid for Fed chair, but she has held firm. Her views on 'moderate restraint' contradict the consensus of most Fed members, who believe that current policy is either in a neutral range or very close. Her voice will become very important over time, as close votes are possible, and she could easily revert to a hawkish stance if prices rebound." (Jinshi)

data.title: Federal Reserve's Bowman Anticipates Robust Economic Growth and Stabilizing Job Market
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, Federal Reserve Governor Michelle Bowman has expressed expectations for the economy to experience "robust" growth. She also anticipates a decline in inflation and a stabilization in the job market.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Bowman Notes Easing Inflation Pressure as Tariff Impact Diminishes
data.description: According to ChainCatcher, Federal Reserve Governor Bowman has indicated that inflationary pressures are gradually easing as the impact of tariffs diminishes.

data.title: Federal Reserve Governor Bowman: Given the current risks, there should be no signal to pause the rate-cutting cycle.
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Bowman stated that given the current risks, the Fed should not signal a pause in its rate-cutting cycle, and monetary policy is currently "moderately tightening." (Jinshi)

data.title: Federal Reserve Governor Bowman: The Fed should be ready to cut interest rates again at any time.
data.description: Federal Reserve Governor Bowman stated that the Fed should be prepared to cut interest rates again, as the impact of tariffs diminishes, inflationary pressures are easing, and the economy is expected to grow "solidly," inflation is declining, and the job market is stabilizing. Congress has granted the Fed independence, enabling it to make difficult decisions. (Jinshi)
